name: Dactyls
description: The Dactyls were primordial, mythical beings in Greek mythology, often associated with metalworking and magic. Their origins are somewhat obscure and vary across different accounts, but they are generally considered to be either children of Gaia (Earth) or offspring of a nymph named Anchiale. They were skilled artisans and were credited with discovering iron on Mount Ida in Phrygia and working with metals to create tools and weapons. Some accounts suggest they were also associated with the mysteries of Samothrace and were considered to be powerful sorcerers and healers. Their number varies significantly depending on the source, ranging from three to several dozen. They are often depicted as being small or dwarfish in stature and are sometimes linked to the Curetes and Corybantes in their role as attendants of Rhea. The Dactyls' knowledge of metallurgy and magic was highly valued, and they were revered as bringers of civilization and technological advancement. They represent the early mastery of metalworking and the connection between the natural world and human ingenuity. They are said to have taught their skills to others, spreading knowledge and progress throughout the ancient world, though their own exact nature and origins remain shrouded in mystery and legend.
subtitle: Metallurgical Masters of Myth
appearance: The Dactyls are typically described as being small or dwarfish in stature. Some accounts depict them as having magical abilities that allow them to shapeshift. They are often associated with the earth and mountains, reflecting their connection to metalworking. Their appearance is not consistently defined, but their small size and association with craftsmanship are recurring features.
habitat: Mount Ida in Phrygia is a primary location associated with the Dactyls, particularly concerning their discovery of iron. They are also connected to the island of Samothrace and the mysteries celebrated there. Their association with mountains and mines suggests a preference for rocky and resource-rich environments.
behavior: The Dactyls are portrayed as industrious and skilled artisans, dedicated to the craft of metalworking. They are credited with teaching humans their knowledge of metallurgy. Some accounts describe them as secretive and protective of their skills, while others emphasize their role as benefactors of humanity. Their behavior is generally characterized by a dedication to their craft and a willingness to share their knowledge with others.
weaknesses: Lack of Iron (dependent on metal resources)

keyMyths.title: Discovery of Iron
keyMyths.description: The Dactyls are credited with discovering iron on Mount Ida in Phrygia, marking a significant advancement in technology. They were the first to learn how to work with this metal, forging tools and weapons that would shape the course of civilization.

sources.author: Diodorus Siculus
sources.description: A comprehensive history of the world from a Greek perspective.
sources.text: A comprehensive history of the world from a Greek perspective.

sources.author: Strabo
sources.description: A detailed description of the ancient world, including geographical and historical information.
